description_long: This sculpture depicts a life-sized rhinoceros rendered in a realistic style, with detailed folds and creases in its thick skin. The animal stands on all four legs atop a rectangular base, its head slightly lowered and turned to one side, showcasing its prominent horn and facial features. The surface texture is rugged, mimicking the natural hide of a rhinoceros, and the coloration is a weathered, earthy brown, suggesting the use of metal or stone. The body is robust and muscular, with clearly defined limbs and a short tail visible from the rear. The sculpture's overall form is solid and massive, giving it a sense of permanence and strength. There are no visible interactive elements, plaques, or signage in the immediate vicinity.
description: A life-sized, realistic rhinoceros sculpture with detailed skin texture and a weathered brown finish, standing on a rectangular base. The piece exudes strength and solidity, with naturalistic anatomical features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rhinoceros's head lowered and horn prominently forward, with both front legs visible and the base extending beneath.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the full profile of the rhinoceros, including the horn, facial features, and the muscular body standing on the base.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the back, tail, and hind legs of the rhinoceros, with the base visible beneath.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the opposite profile, with the horn, head, and body details clearly visible along the length of the sculpture.
attribute_location_context: No specific context is visible; the sculpture appears isolated on a neutral base, suggesting it could be placed in an outdoor urban or park setting.
